admin_panel.notes: The Deco X60 uses 192.168.68.1 (not the standard TP-Link 192.168.0.1) and tplinkdeco.net (not tplinkwifi.net). The web management login password is the TP-Link ID password set during initial Deco app setup — there are no factory default credentials. Only the owner TP-Link ID account can access the web management page. The web panel uses a self-signed HTTPS certificate, so browsers will show a privacy warning — this is normal. The web interface is more limited than the Deco app; WAN settings can be changed via either, but the app provides a more complete configuration experience.

ethernet.pppoe_clear_note: If 'PPPoE' is currently selected with a previous ISP's Username and Password, switch Internet Connection Type to 'Dynamic IP' — the PPPoE credential fields will disappear automatically
steps_ipoe: Connect an Ethernet cable from the nbn connection box (UNI-D or GATEWAY port) to either of the grey Ethernet ports on the back of the Deco X60
Plug in the power cable and wait 2 minutes for the LED to stabilise (pulsing blue means ready for setup, solid green means connected)
Open a web browser and go to http://tplinkdeco.net (or http://192.168.68.1). You must be connected to the Deco's Wi-Fi or plugged into its second Ethernet port
Log in with the TP-Link ID password you set during initial Deco app setup. If you haven't set up the Deco yet, use the Deco app first to complete initial setup
Under the IPv4 section, find 'Internet Connection Type'
Change the connection type to 'Dynamic IP'
Wait 1–2 minutes for the Deco to establish a new internet connection — the LED should turn solid green

wan_config.vlan_notes: Belong does not require VLAN tagging. If IPTV/VLAN is enabled from a previous ISP, disable it: in the Deco app go to More > IPTV/VLAN and turn off IPTV/VLAN. The web interface also supports VLAN configuration.

physical.wan_port_position: Either of the two Ethernet ports on the rear panel — the Deco auto-detects which port has the internet connection
physical.wan_port_notes: The Deco X60 has two identical grey Gigabit Ethernet ports on the rear with no WAN/LAN labeling. Auto WAN Port is enabled by default — whichever port is connected to the internet source (nbn box) automatically becomes the WAN port. The other port serves as a LAN port for wired devices or Ethernet backhaul to another Deco unit.
physical.lan_ports: 1 x Gigabit Ethernet (second port, after WAN is auto-assigned)
physical.reset_button: Recessed pinhole on the bottom of the unit, labeled RESET

factory_reset.method: Turn the Deco X60 upside down and locate the small pinhole labeled RESET on the bottom. Use a pin or paperclip to press and hold the Reset button for 1 second, then release. The LED will turn off briefly and then blink yellow, indicating the reset is in progress. Wait approximately 2 minutes for the unit to restart — the LED will pulse blue when ready for re-setup.
factory_reset.notes: Factory reset erases all settings including Wi-Fi name/password, internet configuration, and mesh network setup. After reset, the Deco must be set up again from scratch using the Deco app — this includes creating or re-linking a TP-Link ID. There are no factory default credentials to restore to; the setup wizard runs again.

firmware.check_path: Advanced > System > Firmware Upgrade (web) or Deco App > More > System > Update Deco
firmware.notes: The Deco X60 supports automatic firmware updates (enabled by default). The Deco app can also trigger manual updates via More > System > Update Deco. For manual firmware files, select the correct hardware version (V1, V2, V3, or V4) from the TP-Link download page before downloading. Firmware can also be manually uploaded via the web interface at Advanced > System > Firmware Upgrade.

setup.setup_notes: The Deco X60 is a mesh Wi-Fi 6 system that is primarily configured through the TP-Link Deco app, though it also has a web admin panel at tplinkdeco.net with more limited functionality. For Belong nbn (IPoE/DHCP), select 'Dynamic IP' as the connection type — this is actually the default option during the initial Deco app setup wizard, so new-out-of-box units typically work without any manual WAN configuration. If the Deco was previously configured for a PPPoE ISP (TPG, iiNet, etc.), you will need to change the connection type to Dynamic IP via the app (More > Internet Connection > IPv4) or web panel. The two Ethernet ports on the rear are identical and auto-sensing — either one can be the WAN port. Note that the Deco uses a different IP subnet (192.168.68.x) and hostname (tplinkdeco.net) compared to standard TP-Link routers.

setup_sources.description: TP-Link official FAQ — how to log in to Deco web management page, confirms 192.168.68.1 and tplinkdeco.net, TP-Link ID authentication
TP-Link AU — Deco X60 setup walkthrough, connection type selection (Dynamic IP/PPPoE), VLAN/IPTV settings for Australia-NBN
MATE (Australian ISP) — step-by-step Deco nbn FTTP setup guide, confirms grey Ethernet ports, 1-second reset, Dynamic IP selection
TP-Link Community — Deco web management UI overview, Advanced menu structure, WAN/IPv4 settings path
TP-Link — how to upgrade Deco firmware, confirms web panel path and Deco app path for firmware updates
TP-Link — how to reset or reboot Deco, confirms pinhole reset button on bottom, 1-second hold duration
TP-Link AU — how to set a fixed WAN port on Deco, confirms auto-sensing WAN/LAN port behavior
Digital Citizen review — rear panel physical layout, two Ethernet ports and power jack, white matte design

setup_confidence.notes: Score: 100 - 5 (admin IP deviates from TP-Link brand default 192.168.0.1, but confirmed by 3+ sources as 192.168.68.1 for Deco line) - 5 (web panel nav path 'Advanced > IPv4 > Internet Connection Type' inferred from community KB and search snippets, not verified with per-model screenshots) - 5 (save button label 'Save' consistent with TP-Link brand default but not directly confirmed from a Deco X60-specific screenshot) - 5 (credential type is user_created via TP-Link ID, which deviates from brand default of standard admin/admin — well-documented but unusual for TP-Link). Physical port layout confirmed by MATE ISP guide (grey ports) and multiple reviews (two identical auto-sensing ports). Factory reset (1 second, bottom pinhole) confirmed by TP-Link official FAQ and MATE guide. Dynamic IP as IPoE label confirmed by TP-Link setup walkthrough and MATE guide.
